fragments.content: Type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM) is an autoimmune disease characterized by T-cell mediated destruction of pancreatic beta cells, resulting in absolute insulin deficiency. Accounts for 5-10% of diabetes cases. Pathogenesis involves genetic susceptibility (HLA-DR3/DR4 haplotypes) combined with environmental triggers (viral infections, early diet, microbiome). Autoantibodies present: GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8, insulin autoantibodies. Onset typically in childhood/adolescence but can occur at any age (LADA - latent autoimmune diabetes in adults). Classic presentation: polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss, diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). Requires lifelong exogenous insulin therapy. Risk of associated autoimmune conditions: thyroid disease, celiac disease, Addison's disease.

Treatment requires intensive insulin therapy via multiple daily injections (MDI) or continuous subcutaneous insulin infusion (CSII/pump). Basal-bolus regimen: long-acting basal insulin (glargine, detemir, degludec) once/twice daily plus rapid-acting bolus insulin (lispro, aspart, glulisine) before meals. Insulin dosing based on carbohydrate counting (typically 1 unit per 10-15g carbs) and correction factor (1 unit lowers glucose by 30-50 mg/dL). Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) strongly recommended; target time-in-range (70-180 mg/dL) >70%, time-below-range <4%. Hybrid closed-loop systems (automated insulin delivery) improve glycemic control and reduce hypoglycemia. Adjunct therapies: pramlintide for postprandial control; SGLT2 inhibitors off-label with caution for DKA risk. Target HbA1c <7% individualized.

Diagnosis confirmed by hyperglycemia criteria (same as T2DM: HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL, random ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms) PLUS evidence of autoimmune beta-cell destruction. Diagnostic workup: measure diabetes autoantibodies (GAD65 most sensitive, IA-2, ZnT8, IAA); low/undetectable C-peptide indicates insulin deficiency. DKA presentation common at diagnosis: glucose >250 mg/dL, pH <7.3, bicarbonate <18 mEq/L, positive ketones. Differentiation from T2DM critical: younger age, lean body habitus, rapid onset, ketosis-prone favor T1DM. Staging system proposed: Stage 1 (normoglycemia + ≥2 autoantibodies), Stage 2 (dysglycemia + autoantibodies), Stage 3 (clinical diabetes). Screening autoantibodies in first-degree relatives considered for prevention trials.
